title: GitHub - fcavalcantirj/claude-code-eyes: Give Claude Code eyes 👁️ — a camera skill so it can SEE real hardware, displays and wiring: verify a rendered panel, check wiring before power-on, and catch bugs that live on the glass, not the logs.
excerpt: Give Claude Code eyes 👁️ — a camera skill so it can SEE real hardware, displays and wiring: verify a rendered panel, check wiring before power-on, and catch bugs that live on the glass, not the log...
content_html:
Give Claude Code eyes. A camera skill so Claude doesn't just read about your hardware — it sees it and drives the whole loop: write the firmware, flash the board, connect the API, look at the display, fix the UI, reflash — verifying its own work where it actually lives: on the glass.

Built by a quadriplegic dev who watched Claude Code wire up an ESP32 and fix its on-screen UI — by looking. This skill is how Claude sees.

Most of what Claude works with is text: code, logs, API responses. But some outputs live where no test can reach them — a font that silently drops characters, a wire in the wrong hole, a number clipped at the edge of an LCD. claude-code-eyes grabs the current camera frame so Claude can read it like any other file — then act on it: edit, reflash, and look again until the real thing is right.

See it in action

▶️ Watch the full 25-second demo — Claude drives the build from the laptop, the board runs the live countdown, and the phone (IP Webcam) is the eye Claude looks through.

🤯 What Claude Code pulled off here — from a prompt, my home Wi-Fi, and the API it would use, and with zero hardware help from me: it designed the board → relay → API architecture, wrote the ESP32 firmware and a Go relay service, flashed the board, shipped 5 clean OTA updates, wrote its own tests (and caught two of its own that were testing nothing) — and found and fixed a UI bug on the display that every green test missed, by looking at the panel through this camera. My whole job: set the goal, plug in the cable, press the button, aim the phone. Claude did the engineering.

Get a camera in 2 minutes (recommended: IP Webcam)

The primary, best-supported camera source is the IP Webcam Android app — it turns any spare Android phone into an HTTP snapshot camera.

  1. Install it from Google Play: 👉 https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.pas.webcam
  2. Open the app, scroll to the bottom, and tap Start server.
  3. The app shows an address on screen, e.g. http://192.168.0.42:8080. That IP:port is your camera URL.
  4. Point the phone at whatever you want Claude to see (a phone stand or a stack of books works). Set the config below to that URL.
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=ipwebcam
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.42:8080      # the IP:port the app shows
# optional, if you enabled "Login/password" in the app:
# export CCE_CAM_AUTH=user:pass

No Android phone? Any camera that can serve a still image over HTTP works — see Camera backends for a Raspberry Pi or a fully generic snapshot URL.

Install

The skill is a folder with a SKILL.md and a snap.sh. Put it where Claude Code looks for skills:

Personal (all your projects):

git clone https://github.com/fcavalcantirj/claude-code-eyes.git \
  ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes

Project-scoped (shared with a repo):

git clone https://github.com/fcavalcantirj/claude-code-eyes.git \
  /path/to/your/project/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes

Or just copy the skill's files into a claude-code-eyes/ folder under either skills/ location. That's the whole skill.

One-command setup

From the installed skill folder, run the setup helper — it writes your camera config and grabs a test frame, so there's nothing to hand-edit:

bash ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes/setup.sh

It asks for your camera URL — or, if you leave it blank, scans your LAN for an IP Webcam and lets you pick one. Prefer non-interactive?

# writes the config and verifies it in one shot (add --auth user:pass if needed)
bash ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes/setup.sh --url http://192.168.0.42:8080 --type ipwebcam

Other flags: --scan (just list IP Webcams on the LAN), --local (write ./.cce.env for this project instead of the global config), --show (where config lives). You can still set the env vars or edit the config by hand — see Configuration.

Then ask Claude to look:

"Are you seeing this? Is the yellow wire in G4?" "Look at the display — does it say ~1h23min in full?" "Watch this — I'm going to press the button."

What it's for

Visual-verify — catch what green tests cannot

A rendered screen is an output no unit test can see. Snap before and after a render change and diff the frames; check the frame against what the spec/API says the screen should show; report the mismatch. It has a small case library baked into the skill: font coverage / dropped characters, text-vs-graphic collisions, clipping at a panel edge, and stale-vs-live renders.

Wiring-mentor — a second set of eyes before power-on

Ask Claude to check a build's wiring against its wiring table before you apply power: it calls out mismatches, verifies polarity and voltage rails (no 5 V on a 3.3 V-only pin), and — importantly — refuses to guess a pin it can't read, asking you to aim the camera closer instead.

Camera backends

Selected with CCE_CAM_TYPE:

CCE_CAM_TYPE Source Request
ipwebcam Android IP Webcam app GET $CCE_CAM_URL/shot.jpg
camera-streamer Raspberry Pi camera-streamer GET $CCE_CAM_URL/snapshot
url (default) Anything that returns a still image over HTTP GET $CCE_CAM_URL (verbatim)

The url mode is the escape hatch: point it at any endpoint that returns a JPEG or PNG (a webcam server, an ESP32-CAM, a signed snapshot URL, ?action=snapshot, etc.). snap.sh verifies the response is actually an image (by magic bytes), so a web page served with 200 OK won't be mistaken for a photo.

# Raspberry Pi camera-streamer
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=camera-streamer
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://raspberrypi.local:8080

# Any snapshot URL
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=url
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.50/cam/snapshot.jpg

Experimental: local USB webcam (untested — verify it yourself)

If you have a USB/built-in webcam and ffmpeg installed, you can grab a single frame directly. This is not shipped as a snap.sh backend because it can't be tested in an automated/headless environment (macOS in particular blocks camera access behind an interactive permission prompt). The portable, supported path is a snapshot URL via one of the backends above. If you want to wire a local camera in yourself, these are the one-liners:

# macOS (avfoundation) — index 0 is usually the built-in camera
ffmpeg -y -loglevel error -f avfoundation -framerate 30 -i "0:none" -frames:v 1 out.jpg

# Linux (v4l2)
ffmpeg -y -loglevel error -f v4l2 -i /dev/video0 -frames:v 1 out.jpg

Grant your terminal camera permission first, run it manually, and confirm out.jpg is a real image before trusting it.

Configuration & precedence

Set config however you like — snap.sh resolves it in this order (first wins):

  1. Environment variables already exported in your shell.
  2. ./.cce.env in the current working directory.
  3. ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/claude-code-eyes/config.

Config files only fill in values you haven't already exported, and they are read, never executed (snap.sh parses KEY=VALUE for a fixed allowlist of keys — a config file cannot run code or set anything else). Example .cce.env:

CCE_CAM_TYPE=ipwebcam
CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.42:8080
# CCE_CAM_AUTH=user:pass
Key Meaning
CCE_CAM_URL Camera URL (interpreted per CCE_CAM_TYPE)
CCE_CAM_AUTH Optional HTTP basic auth user:pass
CCE_CAM_TYPE ipwebcam | camera-streamer | url (default url)

Watch mode

bash snap.sh 3 2     # 3 frames, 2 seconds apart — for "watch this"

Captured frames are written under ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/claude-code-eyes/ and their paths are printed one per line.

Why this exists

I'm a quadriplegic developer. I'm fluent in code, and I knew nothing about electronics or IoT — I'd never wired a board in my life. So I ran an experiment: could Claude Code connect an ESP32 to my system and just… do the whole thing?

It did. Given a goal, my Wi-Fi, and the API it would talk to, Claude designed the architecture, wrote the firmware and a relay service, flashed the board, ran OTA updates, and wrote its own tests. The part I didn't expect: it fixed the UI on the little display. A 48-pixel clock font was silently dropping characters — handed ~1h23min it rendered 1 24m on the panel — and every automated test stayed green, because the bytes really were ASCII; the narrow thing was the font's glyph coverage, which lives on the glass and nowhere else. Claude only caught it by looking at the panel.

And that's where the friction was. Before this skill, "let Claude see the board" meant a soul-crushing manual loop: screenshot the display → send it to myself over WhatsApp → download it → paste it into Claude Code. Every. Single. Iteration. claude-code-eyes deletes that loop — Claude grabs the frame itself, reads it, fixes the code, reflashes, and looks again.

"This is fucking awesome. And yeah — I'm a quad developer."

Honest footnote: I still set the spec, plugged in the cable, and pressed the button — and on one layout bug it was my own eyes that caught what Claude had already marked "pass." Eyes help; they're not infallible. That humility is baked into the skill: it refuses a blurry frame, and it knows what an instrument can and cannot see — a blank frame is not a "no" until the camera is proven to be looking.

License

MIT — see LICENSE.

A camera skill so Claude doesn't just read about your hardware — it sees it and drives the whole loop: write the firmware, flash the board, connect the API, look at the display, fix the UI, reflash — verifying its own work where it actually lives: on the glass.

sentences.zh: 给克劳德·科德一双眼睛。

sentences.en: The app shows an address on screen, e.g. http://192.168.0.42:8080. That IP:port is your camera URL.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序在屏幕上显示地址，例如http://192.168.0.42:8080。该 IP:port 是您的相机 URL。

blocks.html_en: • Point the phone at whatever you want Claude to see (a phone stand or a stack of books works). Set the config below to that URL.

sentences.en: Point the phone at whatever you want Claude to see (a phone stand or a stack of books works).
sentences.zh: 将手机对准你想让克劳德看到的任何地方（手机支架或一堆书都可以）。

sentences.en: Set the config below to that URL.
sentences.zh: 将下面的配置设置为该 URL。

blocks.html_en: No Android phone? Any camera that can serve a still image over HTTP works — see Camera backends for a Raspberry Pi or a fully generic snapshot URL.

sentences.en: No Android phone?
sentences.zh: 没有安卓手机吗？

sentences.en: Any camera that can serve a still image over HTTP works — see Camera backends for a Raspberry Pi or a fully generic snapshot URL.
sentences.zh: 任何可以通过 HTTP 提供静态图像的相机都可以 — 请参阅 Raspberry Pi 的相机后端或完全通用的快照 URL。

sentences.en: Install
sentences.zh: 安装

blocks.html_en: The skill is a folder with a SKILL.md and a snap.sh. Put it where Claude Code looks for skills:

sentences.en: The skill is a folder with a SKILL.md and a snap.sh.
sentences.zh: 该技能是一个包含 SKILL.md 和 snap.sh 的文件夹。

sentences.en: Put it where Claude Code looks for skills:
sentences.zh: 把它放在 Claude Code 寻找技能的地方：

sentences.zh: 个人（您的所有项目）：

sentences.en: Project-scoped (shared with a repo):
sentences.zh: 项目范围（与存储库共享）：

blocks.html_en: Or just copy the skill's files into a claude-code-eyes/ folder under either skills/ location. That's the whole skill.

sentences.zh: 或者只需将技能文件复制到任一技能/位置下的 claude-code-eyes/ 文件夹中。

blocks.html_en: One-command setup

sentences.en: One-command setup
sentences.zh: 一条命令设置

blocks.html_en: From the installed skill folder, run the setup helper — it writes your camera config and grabs a test frame, so there's nothing to hand-edit:

sentences.en: From the installed skill folder, run the setup helper — it writes your camera config and grabs a test frame, so there's nothing to hand-edit:
sentences.zh: 从安装的技能文件夹中，运行设置助手 - 它会写入您的相机配置并抓取测试帧，因此无需手动编辑：

blocks.html_en: It asks for your camera URL — or, if you leave it blank, scans your LAN for an IP Webcam and lets you pick one. Prefer non-interactive?

sentences.en: It asks for your camera URL — or, if you leave it blank, scans your LAN for an IP Webcam and lets you pick one.
sentences.zh: 它会询问您的摄像机 URL，或者，如果您将其留空，则会扫描您的 LAN 中是否有 IP 网络摄像头，并让您选择一个。

sentences.en: Prefer non-interactive?
sentences.zh: 喜欢非交互式？

blocks.html_en: Other flags: --scan (just list IP Webcams on the LAN), --local (write ./.cce.env for this project instead of the global config), --show (where config lives). You can still set the env vars or edit the config by hand — see Configuration.

sentences.en: Other flags: --scan (just list IP Webcams on the LAN), --local (write ./.cce.env for this project instead of the global config), --show (where config lives).
sentences.zh: 其他标志：--scan（仅列出 LAN 上的 IP 网络摄像头）、--local（为此项目写入 ./.cce.env，而不是全局配置）、--show（配置所在的位置）。

sentences.en: You can still set the env vars or edit the config by hand — see Configuration.
sentences.zh: 您仍然可以设置环境变量或手动编辑配置 - 请参阅配置。

blocks.html_en: What it's for

sentences.en: What it's for
sentences.zh: 这是做什么用的

blocks.html_en: Visual-verify — catch what green tests cannot

sentences.en: Visual-verify — catch what green tests cannot
sentences.zh: 视觉验证——捕捉绿色测试无法捕捉到的东西

blocks.html_en: A rendered screen is an output no unit test can see. Snap before and after a render change and diff the frames; check the frame against what the spec/API says the screen should show; report the mismatch. It has a small case library baked into the skill: font coverage / dropped characters, text-vs-graphic collisions, clipping at a panel edge, and stale-vs-live renders.

sentences.en: A rendered screen is an output no unit test can see.
sentences.zh: 渲染的屏幕是单元测试看不到的输出。

sentences.en: Snap before and after a render change and diff the frames; check the frame against what the spec/API says the screen should show; report the mismatch.
sentences.zh: 在渲染更改之前和之后捕捉并比较帧；根据规范/API 规定屏幕应显示的内容检查框架；报告不匹配情况。

sentences.en: It has a small case library baked into the skill: font coverage / dropped characters, text-vs-graphic collisions, clipping at a panel edge, and stale-vs-live renders.
sentences.zh: 它有一个融入技能的小型案例库：字体覆盖/丢弃字符、文本与图形碰撞、面板边缘剪切以及陈旧与实时渲染。

blocks.html_en: Wiring-mentor — a second set of eyes before power-on

sentences.en: Wiring-mentor — a second set of eyes before power-on
sentences.zh: 接线导师——开机前的第二双眼睛

blocks.html_en: Ask Claude to check a build's wiring against its wiring table before you apply power: it calls out mismatches, verifies polarity and voltage rails (no 5 V on a 3.3 V-only pin), and — importantly — refuses to guess a pin it can't read, asking you to aim the camera closer instead.

sentences.en: Ask Claude to check a build's wiring against its wiring table before you apply power: it calls out mismatches, verifies polarity and voltage rails (no 5 V on a 3.3 V-only pin), and — importantly — refuses to guess a pin it can't read, asking you to aim the camera closer instead.
sentences.zh: 在通电之前，要求 Claude 根据接线表检查构建的接线：它会指出不匹配情况，验证极性和电压轨（仅 3.3 V 的引脚上没有 5 V），并且 - 重要的是 - 拒绝猜测它无法读取的引脚，而是要求你将相机瞄准得更近。

blocks.html_en: The url mode is the escape hatch: point it at any endpoint that returns a JPEG or PNG (a webcam server, an ESP32-CAM, a signed snapshot URL, ?action=snapshot, etc.). snap.sh verifies the response is actually an image (by magic bytes), so a web page served with 200 OK won't be mistaken for a photo.

sentences.en: The url mode is the escape hatch: point it at any endpoint that returns a JPEG or PNG (a webcam server, an ESP32-CAM, a signed snapshot URL, ?action=snapshot, etc.).
sentences.zh: url 模式是逃生舱口：将其指向返回 JPEG 或 PNG 的任何端点（网络摄像头服务器、ESP32-CAM、签名快照 URL、?action=snapshot 等）。

sentences.en: snap.sh verifies the response is actually an image (by magic bytes), so a web page served with 200 OK won't be mistaken for a photo.
sentences.zh: snap.sh 验证响应实际上是一个图像（通过魔法字节），因此提供 200 OK 的网页不会被误认为是照片。

blocks.html_en: Experimental: local USB webcam (untested — verify it yourself)

sentences.en: Experimental: local USB webcam (untested — verify it yourself)
sentences.zh: 实验性：本地 USB 网络摄像头（未经测试 - 自行验证）

blocks.html_en: If you have a USB/built-in webcam and ffmpeg installed, you can grab a single frame directly. This is not shipped as a snap.sh backend because it can't be tested in an automated/headless environment (macOS in particular blocks camera access behind an interactive permission prompt). The portable, supported path is a snapshot URL via one of the backends above. If you want to wire a local camera in yourself, these are the one-liners:

sentences.en: If you have a USB/built-in webcam and ffmpeg installed, you can grab a single frame directly.
sentences.zh: 如果您有 USB/内置网络摄像头并安装了 ffmpeg，则可以直接抓取单个帧。

sentences.en: This is not shipped as a snap.sh backend because it can't be tested in an automated/headless environment (macOS in particular blocks camera access behind an interactive permission prompt).
sentences.zh: 它不作为 snap.sh 后端提供，因为它无法在自动化/无头环境中进行测试（特别是 macOS 在交互式权限提示后面阻止相机访问）。

sentences.en: The portable, supported path is a snapshot URL via one of the backends above.
sentences.zh: 可移植的受支持路径是通过上述后端之一的快照 URL。

sentences.en: If you want to wire a local camera in yourself, these are the one-liners:
sentences.zh: 如果您想自己连接本地摄像头，请执行以下操作：

blocks.html_en: Grant your terminal camera permission first, run it manually, and confirm out.jpg is a real image before trusting it.

sentences.en: Grant your terminal camera permission first, run it manually, and confirm out.jpg is a real image before trusting it.
sentences.zh: 首先授予您的终端相机权限，手动运行它，并确认 out.jpg 是真实图像，然后再信任它。

blocks.html_en: Configuration & precedence

sentences.en: Configuration & precedence
sentences.zh: 配置和优先级

blocks.html_en: Set config however you like — snap.sh resolves it in this order (first wins):

sentences.en: Set config however you like — snap.sh resolves it in this order (first wins):
sentences.zh: 按照你喜欢的方式设置配置 - snap.sh 按以下顺序解析它（第一个获胜）：

blocks.html_en: • Environment variables already exported in your shell.

sentences.en: Environment variables already exported in your shell.
sentences.zh: 环境变量已在您的 shell 中导出。

blocks.html_en: • ./.cce.env in the current working directory.

sentences.en: ./.cce.env in the current working directory.
sentences.zh: ./.cce.env 在当前工作目录中。

blocks.html_en: • ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/claude-code-eyes/config.

sentences.en: ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/claude-code-eyes/config.

blocks.html_en: Config files only fill in values you haven't already exported, and they are read, never executed (snap.sh parses KEY=VALUE for a fixed allowlist of keys — a config file cannot run code or set anything else). Example .cce.env:

sentences.en: Config files only fill in values you haven't already exported, and they are read, never executed (snap.sh parses KEY=VALUE for a fixed allowlist of keys — a config file cannot run code or set anything else).
sentences.zh: 配置文件仅填充尚未导出的值，并且它们会被读取，而不会被执行（snap.sh 解析 KEY=VALUE 以获得固定的密钥允许列表 - 配置文件无法运行代码或设置其他任何内容）。

sentences.en: Example .cce.env:
sentences.zh: 示例.cce.env：

blocks.html_en: Why this exists

sentences.en: Why this exists
sentences.zh: 为什么存在这个

blocks.html_en: I'm a quadriplegic developer. I'm fluent in code, and I knew nothing about electronics or IoT — I'd never wired a board in my life. So I ran an experiment: could Claude Code connect an ESP32 to my system and just… do the whole thing?

sentences.en: I'm a quadriplegic developer.
sentences.zh: 我是一名四肢瘫痪的开发人员。

sentences.en: I'm fluent in code, and I knew nothing about electronics or IoT — I'd never wired a board in my life.
sentences.zh: 我精通代码，但对电子或物联网一无所知——我一生中从未对电路板进行过接线。

sentences.en: So I ran an experiment: could Claude Code connect an ESP32 to my system and just…
sentences.zh: 所以我做了一个实验：Claude Code 能否将 ESP32 连接到我的系统，然后……

sentences.en: do the whole thing?
sentences.zh: 做全部事情吗？

blocks.html_en: It did. Given a goal, my Wi-Fi, and the API it would talk to, Claude designed the architecture, wrote the firmware and a relay service, flashed the board, ran OTA updates, and wrote its own tests. The part I didn't expect: it fixed the UI on the little display. A 48-pixel clock font was silently dropping characters — handed ~1h23min it rendered 1 24m on the panel — and every automated test stayed green, because the bytes really were ASCII; the narrow thing was the font's glyph coverage, which lives on the glass and nowhere else. Claude only caught it by looking at the panel.

sentences.en: It did.
sentences.zh: 确实如此。

sentences.en: Given a goal, my Wi-Fi, and the API it would talk to, Claude designed the architecture, wrote the firmware and a relay service, flashed the board, ran OTA updates, and wrote its own tests.
sentences.zh: 给定目标、我的 Wi-Fi 以及与之通信的 API，Claude 设计了架构、编写了固件和中继服务、刷新了主板、运行 OTA 更新并编写了自己的测试。

sentences.en: The part I didn't expect: it fixed the UI on the little display.
sentences.zh: 我没想到的部分是：它修复了小显示屏上的用户界面。

sentences.en: A 48-pixel clock font was silently dropping characters — handed ~1h23min it rendered 1 24m on the panel — and every automated test stayed green, because the bytes really were ASCII; the narrow thing was the font's glyph coverage, which lives on the glass and nowhere else.
sentences.zh: 48 像素时钟字体默默地丢弃字符 — 大约 1 小时 23 分钟后，它在面板上渲染了 1 24 m — 并且每个自动化测试都保持绿色，因为字节实际上是 ASCII；狭窄的地方是字体的字形覆盖范围，它位于玻璃上，而不是其他地方。

sentences.en: Claude only caught it by looking at the panel.
sentences.zh: 克劳德只是看了面板才发现了这一点。

blocks.html_en: And that's where the friction was. Before this skill, "let Claude see the board" meant a soul-crushing manual loop: screenshot the display → send it to myself over WhatsApp → download it → paste it into Claude Code. Every. Single. Iteration. claude-code-eyes deletes that loop — Claude grabs the frame itself, reads it, fixes the code, reflashes, and looks again.

sentences.en: And that's where the friction was.
sentences.zh: 这就是摩擦所在。

sentences.en: Before this skill, "let Claude see the board" meant a soul-crushing manual loop: screenshot the display → send it to myself over WhatsApp → download it → paste it into Claude Code.
sentences.zh: 在这项技能之前，“让 Claude 看板”意味着一个令人心碎的手动循环：屏幕截图→通过 WhatsApp 发送给自己→下载→粘贴到 Claude Code 中。

sentences.en: Every.
sentences.zh: 每一个。

sentences.en: Single.
sentences.zh: 单身的。

sentences.en: Iteration.
sentences.zh: 迭代。

sentences.en: claude-code-eyes deletes that loop — Claude grabs the frame itself, reads it, fixes the code, reflashes, and looks again.
sentences.zh: claude-code-eyes 删除该循环 — Claude 抓取帧本身，读取它，修复代码，重新刷新，然后再次查看。

blocks.html_en: "This is fucking awesome. And yeah — I'm a quad developer."

sentences.en: "This is fucking awesome.
sentences.zh: “这真是太棒了。

sentences.en: And yeah — I'm a quad developer."
sentences.zh: 是的，我是一名四核开发者。”

blocks.html_en: "This is fucking awesome. And yeah — I'm a quad developer."

sentences.en: "This is fucking awesome.
sentences.zh: “这真是太棒了。

sentences.en: And yeah — I'm a quad developer."
sentences.zh: 是的，我是一名四核开发者。”

blocks.html_en: Honest footnote: I still set the spec, plugged in the cable, and pressed the button — and on one layout bug it was my own eyes that caught what Claude had already marked "pass." Eyes help; they're not infallible. That humility is baked into the skill: it refuses a blurry frame, and it knows what an instrument can and cannot see — a blank frame is not a "no" until the camera is proven to be looking.

sentences.en: Honest footnote: I still set the spec, plugged in the cable, and pressed the button — and on one layout bug it was my own eyes that caught what Claude had already marked "pass."
sentences.zh: 诚实的脚注：我仍然设置了规格，插入电缆，然后按下按钮 - 在一个布局错误上，我自己的眼睛发现了克劳德已经标记为“通过”的内容。

sentences.en: Eyes help; they're not infallible.
sentences.zh: 眼睛有帮助；他们并非绝对正确。

sentences.en: That humility is baked into the skill: it refuses a blurry frame, and it knows what an instrument can and cannot see — a blank frame is not a "no" until the camera is proven to be looking.
sentences.zh: 这种谦虚融入到了这项技能中：它拒绝模糊的画面，并且它知道仪器能看到什么和不能看到什么——在证明相机正在观察之前，空白画面并不是“不”。

sentences.en: License
sentences.zh: 执照

blocks.html_en: MIT — see LICENSE.

sentences.en: MIT — see LICENSE.
sentences.zh: 麻省理工学院 — 请参阅许可证。

content_html_zh:
给克劳德·科德一双眼睛。 相机技能让 Claude 不只是读取你的硬件，它还能看到它并驱动整个循环：编写固件、刷新电路板、连接 API、查看显示屏、修复 UI、重新刷新 - 验证其实际工作的位置：在玻璃上。

由一位四肢瘫痪的开发人员构建，他观看了 Claude Code 连接 ESP32 并通过观察修复其屏幕 UI。 这个技能克劳德是这么看的。

Claude 处理的大部分内容是文本：代码、日志、API 响应。 但有些输出存在于任何测试无法到达的地方——一种默默地掉落字符的字体、一根插在错误孔中的电线、一个被夹在液晶显示屏边缘的数字。 claude-code-eyes 抓取当前相机帧，以便 Claude 可以像任何其他文件一样读取它 - 然后对其进行操作：编辑、重新刷新并再次查看，直到真实的内容正确为止。

查看实际效果

▶️ 观看完整的 25 秒演示 — Claude 从笔记本电脑驱动构建，主板运行实时倒计时，电话（IP 网络摄像头）是 Claude 观察的眼睛。

🤯 Claude Code 在这里完成了什么——从提示符、我的家庭 Wi-Fi 以及它将使用的 API，在我的零硬件帮助下：它设计了板 → 继电器 → API 架构，编写了 ESP32 固件和 Go 中继服务，刷新了板，发布了 5 个干净的 OTA 更新，编写了自己的测试（并捕获了两个自己的测试，但没有进行任何测试）——并通过通过这个摄像头查看面板，发现并修复了显示屏上每个绿色测试都错过的 UI 错误。 我的整个工作：设定目标，插上电缆，按下按钮，瞄准手机。 克劳德负责工程设计。

2分钟搞定摄像头（推荐：IP网络摄像头）

主要的、受支持最好的相机来源是 IP Webcam Android 应用程序 - 它将任何备用 Android 手机变成 HTTP 快照相机。

  1. 从 Google Play 安装：👉 https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.pas.webcam
  2. 打开应用程序，滚动到底部，然后点击启动服务器。
  3. 该应用程序在屏幕上显示地址，例如http://192.168.0.42:8080。该 IP:port 是您的相机 URL。
  4. 将手机对准你想让克劳德看到的任何地方（手机支架或一堆书都可以）。 将下面的配置设置为该 URL。
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=ipwebcam
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.42:8080      # the IP:port the app shows
# optional, if you enabled "Login/password" in the app:
# export CCE_CAM_AUTH=user:pass

没有安卓手机吗？ 任何可以通过 HTTP 提供静态图像的相机都可以 — 请参阅 Raspberry Pi 的相机后端或完全通用的快照 URL。

安装

该技能是一个包含 SKILL.md 和 snap.sh 的文件夹。 把它放在 Claude Code 寻找技能的地方：

个人（您的所有项目）：

git clone https://github.com/fcavalcantirj/claude-code-eyes.git \
  ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes

项目范围（与存储库共享）：

git clone https://github.com/fcavalcantirj/claude-code-eyes.git \
  /path/to/your/project/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes

或者只需将技能文件复制到任一技能/位置下的 claude-code-eyes/ 文件夹中。 这就是全部技能。

一条命令设置

从安装的技能文件夹中，运行设置助手 - 它会写入您的相机配置并抓取测试帧，因此无需手动编辑：

bash ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes/setup.sh

它会询问您的摄像机 URL，或者，如果您将其留空，则会扫描您的 LAN 中是否有 IP 网络摄像头，并让您选择一个。 喜欢非交互式？

# writes the config and verifies it in one shot (add --auth user:pass if needed)
bash ~/.claude/skills/claude-code-eyes/setup.sh --url http://192.168.0.42:8080 --type ipwebcam

其他标志：--scan（仅列出 LAN 上的 IP 网络摄像头）、--local（为此项目写入 ./.cce.env，而不是全局配置）、--show（配置所在的位置）。 您仍然可以设置环境变量或手动编辑配置 - 请参阅配置。

然后请克劳德看：

“你看到这个了吗？ 黄线是G4吗？” “看看显示屏——它是否完整地显示了〜1小时23分钟？” “看这个——我要按下按钮。”

这是做什么用的

视觉验证——捕捉绿色测试无法捕捉到的东西

渲染的屏幕是单元测试看不到的输出。 在渲染更改之前和之后捕捉并比较帧；根据规范/API 规定屏幕应显示的内容检查框架；报告不匹配情况。 它有一个融入技能的小型案例库：字体覆盖/丢弃字符、文本与图形碰撞、面板边缘剪切以及陈旧与实时渲染。

接线导师——开机前的第二双眼睛

在通电之前，要求 Claude 根据接线表检查构建的接线：它会指出不匹配情况，验证极性和电压轨（仅 3.3 V 的引脚上没有 5 V），并且 - 重要的是 - 拒绝猜测它无法读取的引脚，而是要求你将相机瞄准得更近。

相机后端

使用 CCE_CAM_TYPE 选择：

CCE_CAM_TYPE 来源 要求
网络摄像头 Android IP 网络摄像头应用程序 获取 $CCE_CAM_URL/shot.jpg
摄像机流光 Raspberry Pi 摄像头流媒体 获取$CCE_CAM_URL/快照
网址（默认） 任何通过 HTTP 返回静态图像的内容 获取 $CCE_CAM_URL（逐字记录）

url 模式是逃生舱口：将其指向返回 JPEG 或 PNG 的任何端点（网络摄像头服务器、ESP32-CAM、签名快照 URL、?action=snapshot 等）。 snap.sh 验证响应实际上是一个图像（通过魔法字节），因此提供 200 OK 的网页不会被误认为是照片。

# Raspberry Pi camera-streamer
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=camera-streamer
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://raspberrypi.local:8080

# Any snapshot URL
export CCE_CAM_TYPE=url
export CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.50/cam/snapshot.jpg

实验性：本地 USB 网络摄像头（未经测试 - 自行验证）

如果您有 USB/内置网络摄像头并安装了 ffmpeg，则可以直接抓取单个帧。 它不作为 snap.sh 后端提供，因为它无法在自动化/无头环境中进行测试（特别是 macOS 在交互式权限提示后面阻止相机访问）。 可移植的受支持路径是通过上述后端之一的快照 URL。 如果您想自己连接本地摄像头，请执行以下操作：

# macOS (avfoundation) — index 0 is usually the built-in camera
ffmpeg -y -loglevel error -f avfoundation -framerate 30 -i "0:none" -frames:v 1 out.jpg

# Linux (v4l2)
ffmpeg -y -loglevel error -f v4l2 -i /dev/video0 -frames:v 1 out.jpg

首先授予您的终端相机权限，手动运行它，并确认 out.jpg 是真实图像，然后再信任它。

配置和优先级

按照你喜欢的方式设置配置 - snap.sh 按以下顺序解析它（第一个获胜）：

  1. 环境变量已在您的 shell 中导出。
  2. ./.cce.env 在当前工作目录中。
  3. ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/claude-code-eyes/config.

配置文件仅填充尚未导出的值，并且它们会被读取，而不会被执行（snap.sh 解析 KEY=VALUE 以获得固定的密钥允许列表 - 配置文件无法运行代码或设置其他任何内容）。 示例.cce.env：

CCE_CAM_TYPE=ipwebcam
CCE_CAM_URL=http://192.168.0.42:8080
# CCE_CAM_AUTH=user:pass
Key 意义
CCE_CAM_URL 相机 URL（根据 CCE_CAM_TYPE 进行解释）
CCE_CAM_AUTH 可选的 HTTP 基本身份验证用户：pass
CCE_CAM_TYPE 网络摄像头 |流光相机|网址（默认网址）

观看模式

bash snap.sh 3 2     # 3 frames, 2 seconds apart — for "watch this"

捕获的帧写入 ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/claude-code-eyes/ 下，并且每行打印其路径。

为什么存在这个

我是一名四肢瘫痪的开发人员。 我精通代码，但对电子或物联网一无所知——我一生中从未对电路板进行过接线。 所以我做了一个实验：Claude Code 能否将 ESP32 连接到我的系统，然后…… 做全部事情吗？

确实如此。 给定目标、我的 Wi-Fi 以及与之通信的 API，Claude 设计了架构、编写了固件和中继服务、刷新了主板、运行 OTA 更新并编写了自己的测试。 我没想到的部分是：它修复了小显示屏上的用户界面。 48 像素时钟字体默默地丢弃字符 — 大约 1 小时 23 分钟后，它在面板上渲染了 1 24 m — 并且每个自动化测试都保持绿色，因为字节实际上是 ASCII；狭窄的地方是字体的字形覆盖范围，它位于玻璃上，而不是其他地方。 克劳德只是看了面板才发现了这一点。

这就是摩擦所在。 在这项技能之前，“让 Claude 看板”意味着一个令人心碎的手动循环：屏幕截图→通过 WhatsApp 发送给自己→下载→粘贴到 Claude Code 中。 每一个。 单身的。 迭代。 claude-code-eyes 删除该循环 — Claude 抓取帧本身，读取它，修复代码，重新刷新，然后再次查看。

“这真是太棒了。 是的，我是一名四核开发者。”

诚实的脚注：我仍然设置了规格，插入电缆，然后按下按钮 - 在一个布局错误上，我自己的眼睛发现了克劳德已经标记为“通过”的内容。 眼睛有帮助；他们并非绝对正确。 这种谦虚融入到了这项技能中：它拒绝模糊的画面，并且它知道仪器能看到什么和不能看到什么——在证明相机正在观察之前，空白画面并不是“不”。

执照

麻省理工学院 — 请参阅许可证。
